powerplant.title: POWER PLANT
powerplant.description: The Bell 407 is powered by a Rolls-Royce model 250-C47B turboshaft engine controlled by a Full Authority Digital Engine Control (FADEC) system with direct reversion to manual mode. The engine drives the main and tail rotors through a transmission. Engine parameters including NG, NP, MGT, and torque are monitored via LCD instruments with trend arcs.
key_points: Engine model: Rolls-Royce 250-C47B
FADEC software version 5.202 required
Takeoff power (5 minutes): 93.5 to 100% torque
Maximum continuous torque: 93.5%
MGT maximum continuous: 727°C (either gauge P/N)
MGT takeoff (5 min): 727 to 779°C
Maximum starting MGT: 927°C (do not exceed 10 seconds above 843°C or 1 second at 927°C)
NG continuous operation: 63 to 105%
NG maximum transient (10 seconds): 105.1 to 106%
NP continuous: 99 to 100%; avoid 68.4 to 87.1%
NP maximum transient (15 seconds): 102.1 to 107%
ENGINE OVSPD light illuminates when NP vs TORQUE exceeds 102.4% NP at 100% TRQ to 108.6% NP at 0% TRQ
Intentional use of transient limits is prohibited
components: FADEC ECU (Full Authority Digital Engine Control)

specifications.notes: Do not exceed 10 sec above 826°C or 1 sec at 927°C
Do not exceed 10 sec above 843°C or 1 sec at 927°C

specifications.item: Engine oil pressure minimum below 79% NG
Engine oil pressure minimum above 94% NG
Engine oil pressure maximum cold starts

fadec_system.title: FADEC FAILURE
fadec_system.description: The Full Authority Digital Engine Control (FADEC) system automatically governs engine power and NP. It operates in AUTO or MANUAL mode. In AUTO mode, the FADEC provides automatic governing; in MANUAL mode, the pilot controls NP via throttle and collective. A direct reversion to MANUAL capability is installed per ASB 407-99-31. FADEC failure may cause an overspeed 2-7 seconds after the FADEC FAIL warning. The PMA goes offline at idle stop during shutdown and may cause engine flameout.
key_points: FADEC software version 5.202 required
AUTO to MANUAL transitions with NR/NP at 100% flat pitch can result in rapid NR/NP acceleration in approximately 7 seconds
FADEC FAIL warning may precede overspeed by 2-7 seconds
FADEC MANUAL mode: no automatic governing; maintain NR with coordinated throttle and collective
FADEC DEGRADED: remain in AUTO mode, fly smoothly and non-aggressively
FADEC FAULT: PMA and/or MGT, NP or NG automatic limiting circuits not functional
RESTART FAULT light: engine cannot be restarted in AUTO mode
PMA goes offline at idle stop; engine may flameout
Manual mode check performed at idle (63% NG) to avoid overspeed
In MANUAL mode NR should be maintained between 95% and 100%
Engine overspeed trip activates at 118.5% NP

specifications.notes: Causes fuel flow to go to minimum
2 to 7 seconds after FADEC FAIL warning

transmission.title: TRANSMISSION
transmission.description: The transmission transfers power from the engine to the main and tail rotors. It is lubricated by an oil system monitored for pressure and temperature. A transmission chip detector monitors for ferrous particles. The transmission oil is cooled by an oil cooler system.
key_points: Transmission oil pressure minimum: 30 PSI
Transmission oil pressure continuous: 40 to 70 PSI
Transmission oil pressure maximum: 70 PSI
Transmission oil temperature continuous: 15 to 110°C
Transmission oil temperature maximum: 110°C
XMSN OIL PRESS warning: reduce power, land as soon as possible
XMSN CHIP caution: land as soon as possible
Recommended oil: DOD-L-85734 to maximum extent allowed by temperature
MIL-L-7808 oil limited to ambient temperatures below -18°C
DOD-L-85734 limited to ambient temperatures above -40°C

rotor_system.title: ROTOR
rotor_system.description: The Bell 407 uses a four-blade semi-rigid main rotor system with a bearingless hub design. The tail rotor is a two-blade teetering type. Rotor RPM is monitored via dual tachometer (NR/NP needles). Rotor RPM limits differ for power-on and power-off conditions. A rotor brake is optionally installed for ground operations.
key_points: Power-on NR continuous: 99 to 100%
Power-off NR minimum: 85%; continuous 85 to 107%; maximum 107%
For autorotative training maintain steady state NR above 90%
RPM warning light (with audio) activates when NR drops below 95% power-on
RPM warning light (without audio) when NR above 107%
Rotor brake limited to ground operation after engine shutdown and NR at or below 40%
Engine starts with rotor brake engaged are prohibited
Rotor tiedowns must be removed before engine start
Tail rotor blades: check tip block security and seal integrity, internal blade root clear of snow and ice

specifications.notes: Normal operation; any time after shutdown for emergency

hydraulic_system.title: HYDRAULIC SYSTEM
hydraulic_system.description: The hydraulic system provides power-assisted flight control actuation for cyclic, collective, and directional control. The system uses MIL-H-5606 fluid and is controlled by the HYD SYS switch. Loss of hydraulic pressure results in increased control forces. A hydraulic systems check is performed before each flight to verify actuator operation. Without hydraulic boost, flight is possible but control forces increase significantly.
key_points: Hydraulic fluid: MIL-H-5606, approved at all ambient temperatures
HYDRAULIC SYSTEM caution light illuminates when pressure is below limit
Loss of hydraulic pressure: reduce airspeed to 70-100 KIAS immediately
Extended flight without hydraulics: up to 120 KIAS to minimize control forces
Run-on landing at ~15 knots recommended after hydraulic failure
Actuator hardover can occur in any flight control axis
Cyclic cam jam only occurs in fore-and-aft axis
HYD SYS switch controls system on/off
Hydraulic check performed before every flight

specifications.notes: MIL-H-5606, approved at all ambient temperatures
70 to 100 KIAS initially; up to 120 KIAS for extended flight

electrical_system.title: ELECTRICAL
electrical_system.description: The Bell 407 has a 28 VDC electrical system with a main generator, battery, and optional GPU capability. The generator is rated for continuous operation up to 180 amps below 10,000 feet Hp and 170 amps above. Transient loads up to 400 amps for 5 seconds are permitted. The battery provides approximately 21 minutes of backup power for basic helicopter and one VHF radio (35 minutes with optional 28 amp-hour battery). The PMA (Permanent Magnetic Alternator) provides power for the FADEC.
key_points: System voltage: 28.5 ± 0.5 VDC nominal (generator on)
Generator continuous below 10,000 ft Hp: 0 to 180 amps
Generator continuous above 10,000 ft Hp: 0 to 170 amps
Generator transient 2 minutes: 180 to 300 amps
Generator transient 5 seconds: 300 to 400 amps
GPU for starting limited to 500 amps (28 VDC)
Battery provides ~21 min power (basic + 1 VHF); 35 min with 28 Ah optional battery
Battery starts demonstrated to -29°C (-20°F) with standard 17 Ah battery, -35°C (-31°F) with optional 28 Ah battery
BATTERY HOT warning: turn BATT switch OFF and land as soon as practical
Left fuel boost/transfer pumps operate directly from battery and continue after BATT switch OFF
GEN FAIL: attempt RESET then ON; if not restored, turn GEN OFF

specifications.item: Generator maximum continuous below 10,000 ft Hp
Generator maximum continuous above 10,000 ft Hp

specifications.notes: Then 30 sec OFF, repeat twice, then 30 min OFF
Then 60 sec OFF, repeat twice, then 30 min OFF

fuel_system.title: FUEL AND OIL
fuel_system.description: The Bell 407 has a fuel system with forward and aft fuel cells. Fuel is transferred from the forward cell to the aft cell by left and right fuel transfer pumps. Fuel boost pumps pressurize fuel to the engine. Basic fuel capacity is 127 US gallons (483 liters); optional auxiliary kit (407-706-011) increases capacity to 147 US gallons (559 liters). A fuel valve controls fuel flow to the engine. Forward and aft fuel sump drains allow preflight contamination checks.
key_points: Basic usable fuel capacity: 127 US gallons (483 liters)
Auxiliary fuel kit capacity: 147 US gallons (559 liters)
Forward cell fuel transfers to aft cell via L and R fuel transfer pumps
Fuel transfer stops at approximately 340 pounds total indicated fuel
Full forward tank (~256 lb) indicated at ~770 lb or greater total fuel
Transfer complete at approximately 185 pounds total fuel
Last 135 pounds in forward cell may not be usable if transfer pumps fail
FUEL LOW caution: 100 ±10 pounds remain in aft tank
Approved fuels at all temperatures: ASTM-D-1655 Type B, MIL-T-5624 JP-4 (NATO F-40)
Approved fuels at -32°C and above: ASTM-D-1655 Type A/A-1, JP-5, JP-8
Left fuel boost/XFR pumps operate on battery even with BATT switch OFF
Airframe fuel filter: drain and check before first flight of day
Critical fuel for most forward CG: 74.8 gallons (283.0 liters)
Critical fuel for most aft CG: 28.4 gallons (107.5 liters)
components: FUEL BOOST/XFR circuit breaker switches (LEFT and RIGHT)

specifications.item: Maximum unusable fuel (both transfer pumps failed)

flight_controls.title: CYCLIC JAM
flight_controls.description: The Bell 407 uses a conventional helicopter flight control system with cyclic, collective, and tail rotor (anti-torque) pedals. Controls are hydraulically boosted under normal operations. The cyclic includes a centering spring capsule (cam) in the fore-and-aft axis. A pedal stop system restricts left pedal travel at high airspeeds; the PEDAL STOP PTT switch on the cyclic controls this function, engaging above 55 ±5 KIAS and disengaging below 50 ±5 KIAS. A cyclic centering light indicates when the cyclic is not centered.
key_points: Cyclic cam jam: only in fore-and-aft axis; requires ~15 lbs force to override spring capsule
Actuator hardover: can occur in any flight control axis
PEDAL STOP engages above 55 ±5 KIAS, disengages below 50 ±5 KIAS
PEDAL STOP caution light: VNE reduced to 60 KIAS; pull PEDAL STOP emergency release
Do NOT press PEDAL STOP PTT switch in flight
Pilot must keep feet on tail rotor pedals at all times
CYCLIC CENTERING light: cyclic not centered
Disregard CYCLIC CENTERING light during run-on or slope landings
Pedal stop disengages with loss of electrical power
Control tube hydraulics-off balance springs maintain feel without hydraulics
components: Pedal stop system (Pedal Restrictor Control Unit)
Hydraulic actuators (cyclic fore-aft, cyclic lateral, collective, directional)

specifications.notes: Approximate force required to override spring

airspeed_limitations.title: AIRSPEED
airspeed_limitations.description: The Bell 407 has a basic VNE of 140 KIAS at sea level to 3,000 feet HD, decreasing with altitude and temperature per the Airspeed Limitations placard. Several conditions reduce VNE to 100 KIAS: takeoff power (93.5-100% torque), lateral CG in shaded area of Figure 1-2, doors removed, steady state autorotation. Maximum sideward/rearward flight or crosswind hover is 35 KTAS. Best rate of climb airspeed is 60 KIAS. Minimum rate of descent in autorotation is 55 KIAS; maximum glide distance airspeed is 80 KIAS.
key_points: Basic VNE: 140 KIAS (sea level to 3,000 ft HD)
VNE decreases with altitude and temperature per placard
VNE reduced to 100 KIAS (not to exceed placarded VNE) at 93.5-100% torque
VNE 100 KIAS with any door(s) removed
VNE 100 KIAS for steady state autorotation
VNE 100 KIAS when lateral CG in shaded area of Figure 1-2
Maximum sideward/rearward flight or crosswind hover: 35 KTAS
Maximum rate of climb: 2,000 feet per minute
Best rate of climb airspeed: 60 KIAS
Autorotation minimum rate of descent: 55 KIAS
Autorotation maximum glide distance: 80 KIAS
Maximum autorotation VNE: 100 KIAS

specifications.item: Basic VNE (sea level to 3,000 ft HD)
VNE at takeoff power or doors removed or autorotation
Autorotation minimum rate of descent airspeed

weight_and_balance.title: WEIGHT AND CENTER OF GRAVITY
weight_and_balance.description: The Bell 407 has a maximum internal gross weight of 5,000 pounds (2,268 kg) for takeoff and landing. With a jettisonable external load, maximum GW is 6,000 pounds (2,722 kg). Minimum GW for flight is 2,650 pounds (1,202 kg). Minimum weight at fuselage station 65.0 is 170 pounds (77.1 kg). Longitudinal CG limits span FS 119.0 to 129.0 inches depending on GW. Lateral CG limits are ±4.0 inches (±102 mm) maximum. The pilot is responsible for computing weight and balance before each flight.
key_points: Maximum internal GW: 5,000 lb (2,268 kg)
Maximum GW with external jettisonable load: 6,000 lb (2,722 kg)
Minimum GW for flight: 2,650 lb (1,202 kg)
Minimum weight at FS 65.0: 170 lb (77.1 kg)
Longitudinal CG limits: 119.0 to 129.0 FS inches (varies with GW)
Lateral CG limits: ±4.0 inches (±102 mm) maximum
Most forward CG fuel: 74.8 gallons (283.0 liters)
Most aft CG fuel: 28.4 gallons (107.5 liters)
Cabin floor load limit: 75 lb/sq ft (3.7 kg/100 cm²)
Baggage compartment load limit: 86 lb/sq ft (4.2 kg/100 cm²), max 250 lb (113.4 kg)
Pilot operates from right crew seat; left seat for additional pilot with approved dual controls

specifications.item: Maximum GW with external jettisonable load

altitude_and_temperature_limits.title: ALTITUDE
altitude_and_temperature_limits.description: The Bell 407 has a maximum operating altitude of 20,000 feet HD or 20,000 feet HP, whichever is lower. Maximum sea level ambient temperature is +51.7°C (+125°F) decreasing at standard lapse rate of 2°C per 1,000 feet. Minimum ambient temperature at all altitudes is -40°C (-40°F). Engine anti-ice must be ON in visible moisture when OAT is below 5°C (40°F).
key_points: Maximum operating altitude: 20,000 feet HD or HP, whichever is lower
Maximum sea level OAT: +51.7°C (+125°F)
Temperature decreases with HP at standard lapse rate: 2°C (3.6°F) per 1,000 ft
Minimum OAT at all altitudes: -40°C (-40°F)
ENG ANTI ICE must be ON in visible moisture when OAT below 5°C (40°F)
Slope landings limited: 10° side slopes, 10° nose up, 5° nose down
Aerobatic maneuvers prohibited
Maximum rate of climb: 2,000 ft/min

rotor_brake.title: ROTOR BRAKE
rotor_brake.description: An optional rotor brake is available for ground operation to stop main rotor rotation after engine shutdown. Normal application is after engine shutdown when NR has decreased to 40% or lower. For emergency stops, the rotor brake may be applied any time after engine shutdown. Engine starts with rotor brake engaged are strictly prohibited. Rapid rotor brake engagement on ice or slippery surfaces should be avoided.
key_points: Normal application: after engine shutdown, at or below 40% NR
Emergency stops: apply any time after engine shutdown
Engine starts with rotor brake engaged are prohibited
Avoid rapid engagement on ice or slippery surfaces
Return rotor brake handle to stowed position just prior to main rotor stopping
Placard: DO NOT APPLY ROTOR BRAKE ABOVE 40% RPM

specifications.notes: At or below this NR for normal ground stop

tail_rotor_system.title: TAIL ROTOR
tail_rotor_system.description: The tail rotor provides anti-torque and directional control. It is driven by a driveshaft from the main transmission through hanger bearings. The tail rotor gearbox contains oil monitored by a sight glass and chip detector. Fixed pitch failures (inability to change blade angle) and complete loss of thrust (drive system break) require different emergency procedures. The vertical fin provides some directional stability at cruise speeds even without tail rotor thrust.
key_points: Complete loss of tail rotor thrust: uncontrollable right yaw, nose-down tuck, possible fuselage roll
In hover with complete loss: close throttle, hovering autorotation
In flight with complete loss: reduce throttle to idle, autorotate at minimum 55 KIAS
Fixed pitch failure hovering: do not close throttle unless severe right yaw occurs
Tail rotor gearbox oil monitored for level and chip particles
T/R CHIP caution: land as soon as possible
Pull pedal stop emergency release for fixed pitch failures
Tail rotor driveshaft hanger bearings: check for grease leakage and overheating
Tail rotor yoke: check for static stop contact damage (deformed yield indicator)

specifications.notes: DOD-L-85734 or MIL-L-7808 per temperature limits

engine_start_system.title: ENGINE START
engine_start_system.description: Engine start is initiated by pressing the START switch which latches the starter. The FADEC manages the start sequence in AUTO mode. A dry motoring run can be performed to reduce residual MGT before start. During cold weather operations, oil pressure and temperature may initially exceed limits but should normalize within approximately 5 minutes at idle. A hot start (excessive MGT) requires keeping the starter engaged to reduce MGT.
key_points: START switch: momentarily press and hold ~1 second; start and AUTO RELIGHT lights illuminate
Starter disengages automatically at 50% NG (START light extinguishes)
AUTO RELIGHT light extinguishes at 60% NG
If main rotor not rotating by 25% NG: abort start by rolling throttle to cutoff
Hot start: keep starter motoring to reduce MGT; abort by rolling throttle to cutoff
Stabilize at idle for 1 minute before increasing throttle if engine shut down >15 minutes
Dry motoring run: hold START switch 15 seconds with throttle in CUT-OFF to reduce residual MGT
Recommended MGT before start: below 150°C (below 10,000 ft HP) or below 65°C (above 10,000 ft HP)
During cold weather: normal limits may be exceeded during start; stabilize at idle
If throttle in idle >60 seconds, starter latching disabled; reposition to cut off then back to idle
Idle: 63 ± 1% NG
Generator should be turned ON after start; battery switch to ON if GPU used

specifications.item: Idle stabilization after restart (>15 min shutdown)

specifications.notes: Hold START switch with throttle in CUT-OFF

engine_anti_ice.title: ENGINE ANTI ICE
engine_anti_ice.description: The engine anti-ice system uses bleed air to prevent ice formation in the engine inlet. It is controlled by the ENG ANTI ICE switch on the overhead panel. When activated, it causes an increase in MGT. An ENGINE ANTI-ICE advisory light (if installed) illuminates when the switch is ON. The system must be ON in visible moisture when OAT is below 5°C (40°F).
key_points: Required ON in visible moisture when OAT is below 5°C (40°F)
Activation causes MGT increase; monitor MGT at high power settings
ENGINE ANTI-ICE white advisory light illuminates when ON (if installed)
If light remains on with switch OFF: avoid maximum power operations
Snow deflectors may be used in conjunction with particle separator
Snow deflector kit (BHT-407-FMS-4) required for falling/blowing snow operations
components: ENGINE ANTI-ICE advisory light (white, if installed)

particle_separator.title: PARTICLE SEPARATOR
particle_separator.description: The optional particle separator kit (206-706-212) separates airborne particles from engine inlet air using a plenum chamber visible through plexiglass windows. Engine bleed air is used to purge debris when the PART SEP switch is ON. Performance is reduced with the particle separator installed, with additional reduction when purge is ON. A power assurance check chart specific to particle separator configuration is provided.
key_points: PART SEP switch ON uses engine bleed air to purge debris
Performance reduced with particle separator installed
Purge ON: reduces gross weight capability by 55 lbs IGE and 50 lbs OGE
PART SEP switch OFF for power assurance check
Check plexiglass windows for snow, slush, or ice accumulation before flight in snow
Can be used with or without snow deflector kit
Particle separator can be removed to restore basic helicopter performance

snow_deflector.title: SNOW DEFLECTOR
snow_deflector.description: The snow deflector kit (206-706-208) consists of two deflectors mounted on either side of the transmission fairing, forward of the engine air inlets. The kit is required for operations in falling or blowing snow. Performance is reduced with snow deflectors installed. Snow deflectors should be removed for operations above 30°C (86°F) and are not recommended above 20°C (68°F).
key_points: Required for operations in falling or blowing snow
Snow deflectors must be removed for operations above 30°C (86°F)
Not recommended above 20°C (68°F) due to performance reduction
Hover in falling/blowing snow limited to 15 minutes, then land and inspect
Flight prohibited when visibility in falling/blowing snow is less than 1/2 statute mile
Compatible with particle separator kit
Install protective covers (engine intake, exhaust, pitot) when leaving helicopter
Power assurance check: level flight only (not hover) with snow deflectors

specifications.notes: Then land and inspect for snow/ice accumulation

fire_detection_system.title: FIRE DETECTION SYSTEM
fire_detection_system.description: The optional fire detection system (kit 407-799-004, 407-706-015, or 407-706-025) illuminates the ENGINE FIRE red warning light on the instrument panel if an excessive temperature or fire develops in the engine compartment. Preflight test: press FIRE DET TEST switch - ENGINE FIRE light should illuminate; release - light should extinguish.
key_points: ENGINE FIRE warning light (red) illuminates on excessive temperature/fire in engine compartment
Preflight test: press FIRE DET TEST switch, verify ENGINE FIRE light illuminates, release and verify extinguishes
ENGINE FIRE in flight: immediately enter autorotation, close throttle, fuel valve OFF, execute autorotation landing, BATT switch OFF
Do not restart engine until cause of fire determined and corrected

quiet_cruise_mode.title: QUIET CRUISE MODE
quiet_cruise_mode.description: The optional quiet cruise mode kit (407-706-016) permits flight at 92% NR to reduce flyover noise by 3.8 dBA SEL. It is enabled by a two-position switch on the collective (QUIET/NORMAL). A two-segment green annunciator (QUIET and ON) indicates mode is active. The mode is limited to VFR operations above 200 feet AGL and 50 KIAS, with a maximum altitude of 6,000 feet HD and maximum internal GW of 5,000 pounds. FADEC software 5.201 or higher required.
key_points: Reduces flyover noise by 3.8 dBA SEL (from 85.1 to 81.3 dBA SEL)
NR in Quiet Cruise Mode: 91.5 to 92.5% (continuous)
Minimum airspeed: 50 KIAS; VNE: 100 KIAS
Minimum altitude: ~200 feet AGL
Maximum altitude: 6,000 feet HD
VFR operations only
Maximum GW: 5,000 lb (2,268 kg)
Torque limited to maximum continuous power (93.5%) in Quiet Cruise Mode
Low rotor audio and RPM light activation reset to 88% NR when QUIET mode selected
FADEC FAIL: QUIET remains illuminated, ON extinguishes; low rotor audio stays at 88%
FADEC MAN mode immediately deselects Quiet Cruise Mode; resets low RPM to 95%
Cargo hook operations in Quiet Cruise Mode not approved
Lateral CG limits in QCM: ±0.9 to 1.4 inches (tighter than basic)
Longitudinal CG aft limit in QCM: FS 127.0 inches

increased_gross_weight.title: INCREASED INTERNAL GROSS WEIGHT
increased_gross_weight.description: The optional increased internal gross weight kit (407-706-020) raises the maximum approved internal gross weight for takeoff and landing to 5,250 pounds (2,381 kg). Maximum HD for takeoff, landing, and IGE maneuvers is limited to 11,000 feet. VNE changes per a new Airspeed Limitations placard. CG limits are expanded accordingly.
key_points: Maximum internal GW increased to 5,250 lb (2,381 kg)
Maximum HD for takeoff/landing/IGE: 11,000 feet (3,353 meters)
New VNE placard provided for 5,250 lb operation
Loads above 5,250 lb must be carried on cargo hook
FAR Part 36 and ICAO certified at 5,250 lb (2,382 kg)
Certified flyover noise level at 5,250 lb: 85.5 dBA SEL
New longitudinal and lateral CG charts provided

specifications.item: Flyover noise level at max GW

noise_levels.title: NOISE LEVELS
noise_levels.description: The Bell 407 is certified as a Stage 2 helicopter under FAR Part 36, Subpart H, and complies with ICAO Annex 16, Volume 1, Chapter 11 noise emission standards. The basic helicopter flyover noise level is 85.1 dBA SEL (FAR Part 36) and 84.6 dBA SEL (ICAO Annex 16). VH for noise calculation purposes is 127 KTAS.
key_points: FAR Part 36 Stage 2 certified at max GW 5,000 lb (2,268 kg)
Basic certified flyover noise level (FAR Part 36): 85.1 dBA SEL
Basic flyover noise level (ICAO Annex 16 Chapter 11): 84.6 dBA SEL
VH for noise calculation: 127 KTAS
No operating limitations to meet noise requirements
ICAO approval applicable only after endorsement by Civil Aviation Authority of country of registration

specifications.item: ICAO Annex 16 Ch.11 flyover noise level

avionics_and_instruments.title: INSTRUMENT MARKINGS AND PLACARDS
avionics_and_instruments.description: The Bell 407 uses LCD instruments with digital readouts and trend arcs for engine parameters (TRQ, MGT, NG, NP, NR). During BATT switch ON, a self-test displays 8188.8 on TORQUE/NG digits and 81888 on MGT/FUEL digits, with NR and NP needles moving to 107% and 100% respectively for 8 seconds. Caution lights include warning (red), caution (amber), and advisory (white/green) categories. An INSTR CHK button allows review of exceedances.
key_points: LCD instruments with trend arcs for engine monitoring
Power-on self-test: 8 seconds with full-scale display
TORQUE/NG display: 8188.8 during test
MGT/FUEL display: 81888 during test
NR to 107%, NP to 100% during test
CHECK INSTR caution: TRQ, MGT, or NG exceedance detected
INSTR CHK button: displays magnitude of exceedance
CAUTION LT TEST button: press to test all caution lights
LCD TEST button available
FADEC HORN TEST button available
CAUT LT switch: DIM dims caution lights to fixed intensity
OAT/VOLTS display for OAT and electrical monitoring
components: LCD instrument displays (TRQ, MGT, NG, NP, NR)
Caution light panel (warning red, caution amber, advisory white/green)
